rows.requirement: Release 2 shared operational shell and navigation state
rows.owner: Release 2 shared UI primitives implementation PR
acceptance: Every hardened surface shows object, scope, freshness, risk/decision point, primary next action, permission state, and result/readback region.
Read-only users see useful context and disabled reasons instead of broken controls.
Surface navigation preserves source/back context for drilldowns.
verification: component tests for shell states and keyboard focus
risks: Shell can become decorative if it does not reserve space for action/result/readback state.

rows.owner: Release 2 shared UI primitives implementation PR
acceptance: Grid supports persisted column widths, order, groups, sticky identity/action columns, loading/fetching/empty/error/stale states, row drilldowns, and reset layout.
Row actions show permission-disabled states and route mutations through governed actions.
Keyboard users can focus rows, headers, drilldowns, and reset layout.
verification: component tests for grid states and layout persistence
E2E-R2-008 where saved view affects runtime surface
risks: Layout storage can drift if local convenience cache is treated as durable tenant configuration.

rows.requirement: KPI strip and signal summary pattern
rows.owner: Release 2 shared UI primitives implementation PR
acceptance: KPI strip shows compact decision metrics, deltas, source/help text, severity, previous-period or baseline comparison, and requires-action count.
Metric drilldowns preserve source context and do not mutate directly.
Severity colors are operational state tokens, not decoration.
verification: component tests for metric, delta, no-previous, and severity states
risks: KPI cards can become passive if not linked to signals and actions.

rows.owner: Portfolio and KPI control implementation PR
acceptance: Highest-risk portfolio row exposes a clear governed next action.
Corrective action, accept risk, request explanation, escalate, and open Gantt actions show permission, preview/result where material, audit, and refresh/readback.
Related project/resource/KPI surfaces refresh or expose source links after action.
non_scope: Executive BI dashboard without action layer
verification: action/API integration tests where DTOs change
risks: Portfolio view can regress into passive status reporting.

rows.requirement: Project Gantt desktop planning surface UI hardening
acceptance: Selected WBS row and timeline bar share one selection state.
Inline date/work edit supports active cell, Enter/Escape, validation, dirty marker, pending save, API readback, audit result, and reload persistence.
Read-only users see plan and disabled reasons.
No duplicated Gantt task entity and no packaged Gantt widget architecture.
verification: unit tests for Gantt edit-state reducer/validation
risks: Visual Gantt can appear complete before persistence/audit/readback is proven.

acceptance: Baseline bars and live bars are visually distinct without copying proprietary UI.
Live edits do not silently mutate baseline values.
Tracking view shows variance, today marker, non-working days, warnings, and audit/readback state.
non_scope: Multiple enterprise baseline families unless promoted by a later task
verification: component tests for tracking overlay states
risks: Baseline overlay can confuse users if dirty/live/persisted states are not explicit.

owned_scope: resource cell drilldown and capacity strip
acceptance: Matrix shows hierarchy rows, sticky time headers, crosshair, heatmap severity, absence/non-working/holiday states, overload/free-capacity states, summary rows, and capacity strip.
Overload cell opens affected work with task/reservation/calendar/source refs.
Read-only state preserves context and explains disabled actions.
verification: component tests for cell states and virtualization boundaries
risks: Heatmap can become decorative if cell drilldown and source refs are weak.

rows.requirement: Resource conflict resolution flow with preview-before-apply
acceptance: Shift, split, reassign, reserve, escalate, and accept-risk paths show preview with before/after load, schedule effects, warnings, blockers, and permission trace.
Apply creates action/audit evidence and recalculates ResourceLoadBucket projection.
Reload keeps resolved/accepted/blocked state visible.
verification: integration tests for preview/apply DTOs where changed
risks: Preview and apply can diverge if result readback is not authoritative.

rows.owner: Portfolio and KPI control implementation PR
owned_scope: KPI source trace and action panels
acceptance: Deviation shows formula version, threshold version, source refs, severity, owner, previous comparison, and recommended action.
Corrective action and accepted risk require governed command and audit; accepted risk requires reason.
Handled status persists and historical evaluations remain stable.
verification: integration tests where action DTOs change
risks: Deviation screens lose trust if source trace is hidden behind generic copy.

rows.requirement: Closed Portfolio / Retrospective control surface hardening
acceptance: Closed snapshot surface shows plan/fact, current vs previous, deltas, quality/CSI where tenant-configured, source trace, and immutable snapshot proof.
Template-improvement action uses preview/result/audit/readback and does not rewrite closed snapshots.
non_scope: Mutable live project analytics as retrospective source
verification: component tests for no-previous and snapshot states
integration tests for improvement action where changed
risks: Retrospective value is lost if snapshot immutability is only implied.

rows.requirement: Tenant Admin saved views/layout/config preview hardening
rows.owner: Tenant admin runtime config implementation PR
acceptance: Admin previews saved view, label, custom field, and KPI threshold runtime impact before publish.
Publish writes versioned audit and affected runtime surface reflects the change after reload.
Invalid configuration is blocked with affected-surface explanation and no partial mutation.
verification: integration tests for versioned config where changed
risks: Admin configuration can feel powerful but unsafe without runtime preview and rollback/readback.

rows.requirement: Release 2 E2E, fixtures, sales-demo quality gate, and exit evidence
acceptance: All R2 rows have evidence and all required R2 scenarios are implemented and passing.
Sales demo path proves first 5-minute clarity and no dead-end screen.
Matrix verification policy is explicit; unsupported verifier shape is either fixed or documented with JSON parse evidence.
non_scope: Adding new Release 2 feature scope during exit
Replacing E2E proof with screenshots only
risks: Exit evidence can become fragmented unless every implementation PR updates the matrix.
